steps.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Warm, moist air is surging northward across the Plains.
This opening step uses the METAR temperature bubbles and keeps the bottom scrubber synced to the METAR timeline.
You can switch to dew point, wind, or pressure using the METAR controls.

steps.title: Moisture Returns
narrative: Switching to dew point helps show the deeper moisture pooling into the region.
This is useful for explaining why storms later become so intense.

steps.title: SPC Upgrade
narrative: The Storm Prediction Center highlights the region for organized severe weather.
Forecasters are increasingly concerned about widespread damaging winds.

steps.title: Forecast Temperatures
narrative: Model temperatures help define the warm sector.
This step now uses the HRRR frame list for scrubbing instead of the lesson minute-by-minute timeline.

steps.title: Instability Builds
narrative: If a CAPE manifest is present, this step will show it.
If no CAPE data has been added yet, this step can be skipped without breaking the demo.

steps.title: Wind Energy Builds
narrative: Forecast wind fields show strong flow feeding the developing system.
These winds help storms organize into a longer-lived structure.

steps.title: Storms Begin to Develop
narrative: Initial storms begin to organize into a more connected line.
Radar is now the best tool for tracking rapid evolution.

steps.title: Warnings Issued
narrative: Severe thunderstorm warnings are issued as storms intensify.
This is when a simulated real-time warning display becomes especially important.

steps.title: Live Doppler View
narrative: A live Doppler-style sweep helps students see how the radar scans the storm.
This is a signature teaching moment in the simulation.

steps.title: Damage Reports Increase
narrative: Storm reports begin to confirm the extent of damaging winds.
These reports are already color-coded by type in the engine.

steps.title: Lightning Peaks
narrative: If lightning data is present in the lesson folder, this step will display it.
This is optional but adds a strong real-time feel to the simulation.

steps.title: Aftermath
narrative: The storm system continues eastward, leaving damage behind.
This sequence sets up the recap section and future damage-photo steps.
